title: mc
text: Mail Composer — compose, draft, send emails markdown via Gmail API. Wraps gmailr reduce email scripts 80 lines boilerplate 3.

title: mc
text: Repository: NewGraphEnvironment/mc Primary Language: R License: MIT Status: Early development (0.0.0.9000)

title: mc
text: R/ — Package source: mc_auth, mc_compose, mc_md_render, mc_scroll, mc_send, mc_sig, mc_thread_find man/ — roxygen2-generated docs vignettes/ — pkgdown vignettes (tables--emails) tests/testthat/ — testthat v3 tests data-raw/ — Data preparation scripts inst/ — Installed files (signature template, etc.) dev/ — Development scripts

title: mc
text: Markdown envelope --- stripped mc_md_render() HTML conversion mc_compose() accepts mixed markdown files, raw HTML, kable objects mc_scroll() wraps HTML tables horizontal/vertical scrolling email clients Input validation uses chk package exported functions HTML conversion via commonmark Gmail API interaction via gmailr

title: mc
text: devtools::document() rebuild NAMESPACE man pages devtools::test() run tests pkgdown::build_site() docs site Follow tidyverse/r-lib conventions (roxygen2 markdown, testthat v3)

title: mc
text: repo GitHub Actions workflows, scan recent runs session start. Catches failed pkgdown deploys, broken vignette builds, stale citation regenerations otherwise linger user manually checks.

title: mc
text: failures since last visit, surface user starting work: Workflow <name> failed <time> ago (run <id>). Investigate gh run view <id> --log-failed. Fix proceed current task? User decides; auto-fix.
code: gh run list --limit 5 --json status,conclusion,name,createdAt,databaseId \   --jq '.[] | select(.conclusion == "failure")'

title: mc
text: pkgdown — docs site GitHub Pages broken R-CMD-check — package may install Vignette / build-vignettes — vignette docs incomplete update-citation-cff — CITATION.cff stale

title: mc
text: Without scan, post-merge workflow failures linger someone (often user) notices stale docs site missing vignette. session-start sweep catches first re-entry repo.

title: mc
text: skill watches workflows triggered fresh merge real time — ’s targeted catch. convention backstop failures landed one watching (merges via web UI, scheduled triggers, manually-triggered workflows).

title: mc
text: Structured checklist reviewing diffs commit. Used /code-check. Add new checks bug class discovered — compound time.

title: mc
text: Variables double-quoted strings containing single quotes break value ' "echo '${VAR}'" — VAR contains ', shell syntax breaks Use printf '%s\n' "$VAR" | command pipe values safely Heredocs: unquoted <<EOF expands variables locally, <<'EOF' — know need Pass--ssh args: printf '%q' escapes per-arg workload paths spaces / quotes / metacharacters survive local-shell → ssh-argv → remote-shell round-trip. Without , ssh host 'cmd' "$path" joins args spaces remote re-parses, losing argument boundaries.

title: mc
text: cmd1 | cmd2 <<EOF — heredoc binds cmd2 (rightmost simple command). intended cmd1 receive , put <<EOF cmd1 explicitly: cmd1 <<EOF | cmd2. Symptom wrong: ssh body silently echoed tee/cat/etc, ssh side gets empty stdin, exits 0 (near-0) without anything. Caught hard way 2026-05-01 cypher_restore-fwapg.sh.

title: mc
text: set -eu propagate exit codes pipelines. ssh ... | tee log returns tee’s exit (always 0 healthy tee), masking ssh failure. Use set -euo pipefail script pipes meaningful command tee/cat/grep/etc. check ${PIPESTATUS[0]} explicitly. Symptom wrong: task notifications report “exit 0 / completed” remote work actually skipped errored.

title: mc
text: Hardcoded absolute paths (/Users/airvine/...) break users Use REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/<relative>" && pwd)" moving scripts, verify ../ depth still resolves correctly Usage comments match actual script location

title: mc
text: || true hides real errors — failure actually safe ignore? Empty variable destructive operation (rm, destroy) — add guard: [ -n "$VAR" ] || exit 1 grep returning empty silently — downstream commands get empty input

title: mc
text: Secrets passed command-line args visible ps aux Use env files, stdin pipes, temp files chmod 600 instead

title: mc
text: Must pure ASCII — em dashes, curly quotes, arrows cause silent parse failure Check : perl -ne 'print "$.: $_" /[^\x00-\x7F]/' file.yaml

title: mc
text: runcmd item containing { : risk parsed YAML flow-mapping (dict), literal string. Cloud-init’s shellify hits non-string throws TypeError, aborting subsequent runcmd steps silently final_message still fires. Don’t write: - test -s /file || { echo "FATAL: ..." } — : inside braces makes YAML see dict. write: use - | block scalar explicit //fi: Validate post-edit: python3 -c "import yaml; runcmd=yaml.safe_load(open('cloud-init.yaml').read().split(chr(10),1)[1])['runcmd']; print([type(x).__name__ x runcmd isinstance(x,str)] 'strings')". output anything strings, runcmd fail.
code: - |   if [ ! -s /file ]; then     echo "FATAL: ..." >&2     exit 1   fi

title: mc
text: cloud-init clean causes full re-provisioning next boot — almost never want snapshot Use tailscale logout tailscale snapshot (deregister vs disconnect) Wipe /var/lib/tailscale/* snapshot — tailscale logout deauthorizes server-side local node identity blob persists tailscaled.state. Snapshot restored elsewhere inherits prior key material tailscale runs . Wipe /etc/ssh/ssh_host_* snapshot — otherwise droplets spawned image share host identity.

title: mc
text: Secrets rendered via templatefile() readable 169.254.169.254 metadata endpoint Acceptable ephemeral machines, document tradeoff Heredocs runcmd write secrets: <<'EOF' (quoted) prevents bash re-expanding $X sequences already-substituted credential strings. AWS keys rarely contain $ base64-padded secrets might.

title: mc
text: apt-key adv --keyserver deprecated Ubuntu 24.04 noble — silently fails APT ignores resulting keyring. Use gpg --dearmor + signed-= keyring file pattern. Repo .list files write_files: trigger implicit package_update runcmd installs keyring → first apt-get update fails NO_PUBKEY. Put repo line runcmd alongside key install, write_files.

what: Cloud-init users vs DO SSH key injection
title: mc
text: injects ssh_key_ids /root/.ssh/authorized_keys (cloud-init’s cc_ssh module). Cloud-init users: block ssh_authorized_keys: [] pick . Non-root users need SSH access must copy root’s keys runcmd: Guard test -s /root/.ssh/authorized_keys fail loudly cc_ssh hasn’t run runcmd (rare race).
code: - mkdir -p /home/<user>/.ssh - cp /root/.ssh/authorized_keys /home/<user>/.ssh/authorized_keys - chown -R <user>:<user> /home/<user>/.ssh

title: mc
text: Parsing tofu state show text output fragile — use tofu output instead Missing outputs scripts need — add main.tf Snapshot/image IDs tfvars deleting snapshot — stale reference

title: mc
text: Validate resource IDs destroy: [ -n "$ID" ] || exit 1 tofu destroy without -target destroys everything including reserved IPs Snapshot ID extraction name: use awk -v n="$NAME" '$2 == n {print $1}' (exact match column 2). grep -F "$NAME" substring-match can grab stale snapshot whose name contains new name substring.

title: mc
text: snapshots include source droplet’s disk size. New droplets snapshot must disk >= snapshot disk. Resize fine; resize snapshot disk impossible without rebuilding. Build snapshot smallest droplet size ’d ever want spin . Sizes vs disks writing: g-4vcpu-16gb = 50 GB, g-8vcpu-32gb / m-4vcpu-32gb = 100 GB, m-8vcpu-64gb = 200 GB. workload requires X GB RAM minimum, snapshot floor whatever droplet X GB smallest disk class.

title: mc
text: Targeted destroy (tofu destroy -target=module.droplet -target=...assignment...) preserves reserved IP $4/mo. Full tofu destroy releases (next apply gets NEW IP).

title: mc
text: returns 422 “Droplet already pending event” reserved IP assignment fires immediately droplet+firewall creation. droplet’s internal event queue takes time drain. time_sleep resource droplet creation IP assignment, create_duration ≥ 60s (10s 30s observed race; 60s headroom) depends_on = [time_sleep.<name>] digitalocean_reserved_ip_assignment resource retry fallback wrapping shell script (.sh style) detects 422 tofu output uses doctl compute reserved-ip-action assign <ip> <droplet-id> recover. Tofu doesn’t retry; leaves state half-applied (assignment recorded didn’t actually attach). Snapshot-based spins prone race first-boot blank Ubuntu (startup events compete droplet’s event queue). Audit existing modules: grep -L 'time_sleep' env//*/<host>/main.tf finds modules missing gate. 2026-05-02, openclaw geoserv time_sleep — race eventually.

title: mc
text: imresamu/postgis (similar postgis images) first cold start (empty data volume) take 5-12 min install extensions — varies disk IO noisy-neighbor lottery cloud hosts. Health-wait scripts must allow 15 min minimum, ideally hard-fail + log dump timeout.

title: mc
text: fresh’s docker/docker-compose.yml defaults tuned 128 GB host (shared_buffers=32GB, shm_size=36gb). smaller hosts, postgres OOMs startup “map anonymous shared memory”. 32 GB host floor: use M1/cypher 32 GB-host preset (scripts/fwapg/compose.override.m1.yml) sets shared_buffers=8GB, shm_size=12gb. 32 GB: postgres can technically start smaller shared_buffers fwapg work becomes painful. Don’t run fwapg pipelines <32 GB hosts.

title: mc
text: ALTER DATABASE <db> SET search_path ... database-level setting stored postgres data dir. Wiped docker compose -v. Must re-applied every restore. Codify restore script, cloud-init compose env (don’t apply db-level settings).

title: mc
text: Tailscale SSH ACL "users": ["autogroup:nonroot"] tag:compute blocks ssh root@<node> tailnet. Use ssh <user>@<node> + sudo root operations. SSH--root -tailnet (regular OpenSSH public IP), ACL doesn’t apply — need SSH key registered node.

title: mc
text: Cypher-style ephemeral compute droplets need flags auth key: Reusable (key works across destroy/recreate) + Ephemeral (tailnet entries auto-clean offline >5 min). Tag key (e.g. tag:compute) creation time. Nodes joining key inherit tag automatically — --advertise-tags needed tailscale time.

title: mc
text: .tfvars must gitignored (contains tokens, passwords) .tfvars.example variables empty/placeholder values Sensitive variables need sensitive = true variables.tf

title: mc
text: 0.0.0.0/0 SSH world-open — document intentional access gated Tailscale, say explicitly

title: mc
text: Passwords special chars (', ", $, !) break naive shell quoting printf '%q' escapes values shell safety Temp files secrets: create chmod 600, delete use

title: mc
text: pak stops first unresolvable package — subsequent packages skipped Removed CRAN packages (like leaflet.extras) must move GitHub source PPPM binaries may lag hours behind new CRAN releases

title: mc
text: Branch pins (pkg@branch) reproducible — document used Pinned download URLs (RStudio .deb) go stale — document update

title: mc
text: importing config one location canonical one (legacy ~/.bash_profile → dotfiles repo, old script’s env → repo, another project’s settings.json → soul): Verify every referenced path/binary exists. Dead PATH exports, missing interpreters, stale env vars cut, codified. Shell paths: p $(echo "$PATH" | tr ':' ' '); [ -d "$p" ] || echo "DEAD: $p"; done Ask dropping reference — may something user forgot reinstall machine, something delete. Curated subset, verbatim copy. diff reflect verified, whole source.

title: mc
text: Moving/renaming scripts: update CLAUDE.md, READMEs, usage comments New variables: update .tfvars.example New workflows: update relevant README

title: mc
text: non-trivial issue-driven work, follow checklist. step exists reason — skipping leads rework, broken builds, avoidable bugs ’ve hit repeatedly.

title: mc
text: Start /planning-init <N> — given issue number, enters plan mode codebase exploration, presents phase breakdown user approval, scaffolds branch + PWF baseline approved phases. One command replaces manual issue → explore → plan → branch → scaffold dance. Write robust tests first — failing tests reproduce issue document new behavior. Tests contract; fail work makes pass. Name intent — functions, parameters, internal helpers carry naming style package live . Look existing exports guide; consistency cleverness. (Per-package naming convention TBD — see soul issue tracking.) Examples run — every exported function gets runnable @examples block. Pkgdown renders ; CI executes . example doesn’t run documentation rot. Code-check commit — /code-check staged diff. Catches tests miss: edge cases, hard-coded paths, unguarded variables, security issues. Atomic commits — commit bundles code change + checkbox flip task_plan.md. diff progress live commit; git log -- planning/ tells full story. /planning-archive complete — moves PWF archive/YYYY-MM-issue-N-slug/, creates fresh active/. /gh-pr-push opens PR; /gh-pr-merge handles release bookkeeping.

title: mc
text: one-line typo fixes, version-bump-PRs, trivial documentation edits, full workflow overhead. Use judgment. threshold roughly: multi-step issue, multi-file change, anything requires scoping → use workflow.

title: mc
text: /planning-init <N> — start /planning-update — sync checkboxes mid-session /code-check — every commit /planning-archive — issue closes /gh-pr-push — open PR /gh-pr-merge — merge release bookkeeping

title: mc
text: ’ve hit snags repeatedly half-— branches mix concerns, tests bolted , code-check skipped (bug ships diff), examples fail pkgdown. step small; cumulative reliability gain real. convention becomes default expectation, thing user remind every session .

title: mc
text: Behavioral guidelines reduce common LLM coding mistakes. Merge project-specific instructions needed. Tradeoff: guidelines bias toward caution speed. trivial tasks, use judgment.

title: mc
text: Don’t assume. Don’t hide confusion. Surface tradeoffs. implementing: - State assumptions explicitly. uncertain, ask. - multiple interpretations exist, present - don’t pick silently. - simpler approach exists, say . Push back warranted. - something unclear, stop. Name ’s confusing. Ask.

title: mc
text: Minimum code solves problem. Nothing speculative. features beyond asked. abstractions single-use code. “flexibility” “configurability” wasn’t requested. error handling impossible scenarios. write 200 lines 50, rewrite . Ask : “senior engineer say overcomplicated?” yes, simplify.

title: mc
text: Touch must. Clean mess. editing existing code: - Don’t “improve” adjacent code, comments, formatting. - Don’t refactor things aren’t broken. - Match existing style, even ’d differently. - notice unrelated dead code, mention - don’t delete . changes create orphans: - Remove imports/variables/functions changes made unused. - Don’t remove pre-existing dead code unless asked. test: Every changed line trace directly user’s request.

title: mc
text: Define success criteria. Loop verified. Transform tasks verifiable goals: - “Add validation” → “Write tests invalid inputs, make pass” - “Fix bug” → “Write test reproduces , make pass” - “Refactor X” → “Ensure tests pass ” multi-step tasks, state brief plan: Strong success criteria let loop independently. Weak criteria (“make work”) require constant clarification. guidelines working : fewer unnecessary changes diffs, fewer rewrites due overcomplication, clarifying questions come implementation rather mistakes.
code: 1. [Step] → verify: [check] 2. [Step] → verify: [check] 3. [Step] → verify: [check]

title: mc
text: Claude manages structured planning complex tasks using planning--files (PWF).

title: mc
text: Use PWF task multiple phases, requires research, involves ~5 tool calls. Triggers: - User says “let’s plan ”, “plan mode”, “use planning”, invokes /planning-init - Complex issue work begins (multi-step, uncertain approach) - Claude judges task warrants structured tracking Skip planning single-file edits, quick fixes, tasks obvious next steps.

title: mc
text: Explore first — Enter plan mode (read-). Read code, trace paths, understand problem proposing anything. task_plan.md — Phases checkbox tasks findings.md — Research, discoveries, technical analysis progress.md — Session log timestamps commit refs Plan-review Plan agent committing plan — scaffolding task_plan.md baseline commit, spawn Plan subagent (Agent({subagent_type: "Plan", prompt: "..."}) ask critically review task_plan issue body + actual codebase. Categorize findings Blocker / Gap / Ordering / Assumption / Scope / Acceptance. Address committing. agent reads files fresh — catches miss ’ve thinking design long. Real example: caught 21 issues including hardcoded literals across 4 files listed plan, untested DB column mismatches, unfixable test-literal-string assertions, baseline-cache-shadow produced 6-second -op run. Cost: ~5 min agent. Saves: hours mid-implementation rework. Commit plan — Plan-agent review + fixes. baseline. Work atomic commits — commit bundles code changes checkbox updates planning files. diff shows done checkbox marking done. Code check commit — Run /code-check staged diffs committing. Don’t mark task done diff passes review. Archive complete — Move planning/active/ planning/archive/ via /planning-archive. Write README.md archive directory one-paragraph outcome summary closing commit/PR ref — future sessions scan catch fast.

title: mc
text: Every commit completes planned task MUST include: - code/script changes - checkbox update task_plan.md (- [ ] -> - [x]) - progress entry progress.md meaningful creates git audit trail git log -- planning/ tells full story. commit self-documenting — can backtrack git understand everything happened.

title: mc
text: Phases checkboxes. core tracking file. Mark tasks done ’re completed: - [x] Task description
code: # Task Plan  ## Phase 1: [Name] - [ ] Task description - [ ] Another task  ## Phase 2: [Name] - [ ] Task description

title: mc
text: Append-research log. Discoveries, technical analysis, things learned.
code: # Findings  ## [Topic] [What was found, with source/date]

title: mc
text: Session entries commit references.
code: # Progress  ## Session YYYY-MM-DD - Completed: [items] - Commits: [refs] - Next: [items]

title: mc
text: planning/ doesn’t exist repo, run /planning-init first.
code: planning/   active/          <- Current work (3 PWF files)   archive/         <- Completed issues     YYYY-MM-issue-N-slug/

title: mc
text: Standards R package development across New Graph Environment repositories. Based R Packages (2e) Hadley Wickham Jenny Bryan. Reference packages: starting new package, study existing packages patterns: flooded, gq. demonstrate conventions practice (DESCRIPTION fields, README layout, NEWS.md style, pkgdown setup, test structure, hex sticker, etc.).

title: mc
text: tidyverse style guide: snake_case, pipe operators (|> %>%) Match existing patterns codebase Use pak package installation (install.packages) Prefix column name vectors cols_ discoverability environment pane: cols_all, cols_carry, cols_split, cols_writable. principle grouped vectors (params_, tbl_, etc.) SQL DDL+INSERT pairs share schema, use single named vector source truth. CREATE TABLE INSERT (cols) SELECT cols derive column lists cols_* vector. Avoids drift table shape write projection — columns change, edit one place. Example:
code: cols_streams <- c(   id_segment           = "integer NOT NULL",   watershed_group_code = "varchar(4) NOT NULL",   geom                 = "geometry(MultiLineStringZM, 3005)"   # … ) # CREATE TABLE consumes both names + types ddl_body <- paste(names(cols_streams), unname(cols_streams), sep = " ",                   collapse = ", ") # INSERT consumes names only proj <- paste(names(cols_streams), collapse = ", ")

title: mc
text: Follow R Packages (2e) conventions: - R/ functions, tests/testthat/ tests, man/ docs - DESCRIPTION proper fields (Title, Description, Authors@R) - DESCRIPTION URL field: include GitHub repo pkgdown site pkgdown links correctly (e.g., URL: https://github.com/OWNER/PKG,   https://owner.github.io/PKG/) - NAMESPACE managed roxygen2 (#' @export, #' @import, #' @importFrom) - Never edit NAMESPACE man/ hand

title: mc
text: exported function gets R file test file: - R/fl_mask.R → tests/testthat/test-fl_mask.R - Commit function tests together - Use Fixes #N commit message close corresponding issue

title: mc
text: File GitHub issue function building . creates traceable record planned, built, verified.

title: mc
text: new packages major features, work branch merge via PR: gives one PR contains commits — single SRED cross-reference covers entire body work. Individual commits within branch close respective function issues Fixes #N.
code: main ← scaffold-branch (PR closes with "Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred#N")

title: mc
text: Close function issues via commit messages — see Closing Issues newgraph conventions.

title: mc
text: Use testthat 3e (Config/testthat/edition: 3 DESCRIPTION) Run devtools::test() committing Test files mirror source: R/utils.R -> tests/testthat/test-utils.R Test edge cases potential failures, just happy paths Tests must pass closing function’s issue Always grep errors command test run avoid running twice: error context: grep -E "(ERROR:|FAIL )" -10 | head -25
code: Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' 2>&1 | grep -E "(FAIL|ERROR|PASS)" | tail -5

what: Runnable examples on every exported function
title: mc
text: Examples users discover function . must: - Actually run — \dontrun{} unless external resources required - Use bundled test data via system.file() work anyone - Show function useful — just runs, produces ’d use - Use qualified names non-exported dependencies (terra::rast(), sf::st_read()) since examples run user’s environment

title: mc
text: least one vignette showing full pipeline real data: - Demonstrates package solving actual problem end--end - Uses bundled test data (committed inst/testdata/) - Hosted pkgdown users can read without installing Output format: Use bookdown::html_vignette2 (rmarkdown::html_vignette) figure numbering cross-references. Requires bookdown Suggests chunks must fig.cap numbered figures. Cross-reference Figure \@ref(fig:chunk-name). Vignettes need external resources (DB, API, STAC): use .Rmd.orig pre-knit pattern — breaks bookdown figure numbering knitr evaluates chunks pre-knit emits ![](path) markdown bookdown can’t number. Instead, separate data generation presentation: 1. data-raw/vignette_data.R — runs queries, saves results .rds inst/testdata/ (inst/vignette-data/) 2. Vignette loads .rds files, chunks run live pkgdown build 3. Note top vignette: “Data generated data-raw/script.R” 4. bookdown controls chunks — figure numbers, cross-refs work pattern test data: data-raw/ documents data produced, committed artifacts make vignettes reproducible without external resource.

title: mc
text: Created via script data-raw/ documents exactly data produced (database queries, spatial crops, etc.) Committed inst/testdata/ — small enough ship package Used tests, examples, vignettes — one dataset, three purposes

title: mc
text: roxygen2 exported functions @import @importFrom package-level doc (R/<pkg>-package.R) populate NAMESPACE — don’t rely :: everywhere function bodies pkgdown site public packages _pkgdown.yml (bootstrap 5) GitHub Action pkgdown (usethis::use_github_action("pkgdown"))

title: mc
text: Run lintr::lint_package() committing R package code. Fix warnings — every lint worth fixing.

title: mc
text: 120 char line length (default 80 strict data pipelines) Allow dotted.case (common base R legacy code) Suppress commented code lints (exploratory R scripts often commented alternatives) Exclude renv directory entirely
code: linters: linters_with_defaults(     line_length_linter(120),     object_name_linter(styles = c("snake_case", "dotted.case")),     commented_code_linter = NULL   ) exclusions: list(     "renv" = list(linters = "all")   )

title: mc
text: Minimize Imports — use Suggests packages needed tests/vignettes Pin versions breaking changes known Prefer packages already tidyverse ecosystem

title: mc
text: First release: one line (e.g., “Initial release. Brief description.”) Later releases: describe changed , function--function. Link pkgdown reference page details — don’t duplicate . Don’t list every function; pkgdown reference page single source truth ’s package. Bump version DESCRIPTION (e.g., 0.0.0.9000 → 0.1.0) — final commit branch, verification numbers/tests final. Mid-branch bumps premature churn: additional code changes end bundled inside “release” already claimed version. Commit “Release vX.Y.Z” Tag: git tag vX.Y.Z && git push && git push --tags

title: mc
text: Protect main deletion force pushes:
code: gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/rulesets --method POST --input - <<'EOF' {   "name": "Protect main",   "target": "branch",   "enforcement": "active",   "bypass_actors": [     { "actor_id": 5, "actor_type": "RepositoryRole", "bypass_mode": "always" }   ],   "conditions": { "ref_name": { "include": ["refs/heads/main"], "exclude": [] } },   "rules": [ { "type": "deletion" }, { "type": "non_fast_forward" } ] } EOF

title: mc
text: usethis::create_package(".") usethis::use_mit_license("New Graph Environment Ltd.") usethis::use_testthat(edition = 3) usethis::use_pkgdown() usethis::use_github_action("pkgdown") usethis::use_directory("dev") — reproducible setup script usethis::use_directory("data-raw") — data generation scripts Hex sticker via hexSticker (see data-raw/make_hexsticker.R) Set GitHub Pages serve gh-pages branch

title: mc
text: Keep dev/dev.R file documents every setup step. idempotent — run interactively. reproducible recipe package scaffold.

title: mc
text: Keep README lean: - Hex sticker, one-line description, install, example showing ’s useful - Link pkgdown vignette function reference — don’t duplicate - Don’t maintain function table — ’s just another thing keep updated pkgdown’s reference page single source truth

title: mc
text: LLM assistant modifies R package code: 1. Run lintr::lint_package() — fix issues committing 2. Run devtools::test() error grep — ensure tests pass one call: bash    Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' 2>&1 | grep -E "(FAIL|ERROR|PASS)" | tail -5 3. Run devtools::document() grep results: bash    Rscript -e 'devtools::document()' 2>&1 | grep -E "(Writing|Updating|warning)" | tail -10 4. Check devtools::check() passes releases — capture results one call: bash    Rscript -e 'devtools::check()' 2>&1 | grep -E "(ERROR|WARNING|NOTE|errors|warnings|notes)" | tail -10

title: mc
text: references flow Claude Code, Zotero, technical writing New Graph Environment.

title: mc
text: Three tools, different purposes. Use right one. Citation keys vs item keys: Citation keys (like irvine2020ParsnipRiver) come Better BibTeX. Item keys (like K7WALMSY) native Zotero. MCP works item keys. /zotero-lookup bridges citation keys item data. BBT citation key storage: Feb 2025+, BBT stores citation keys citationKey field directly zotero.sqlite (via Zotero’s item data system), separate BBT database. old better-bibtex.sqlite better-bibtex.migrated files stale longer updated. Query citation keys : SELECT idv.value items JOIN itemData id .itemID = id.itemID JOIN itemDataValues idv id.valueID = idv.valueID JOIN fields f id.fieldID = f.fieldID f.fieldName = 'citationKey'.

title: mc
text: research turns reference: - DOI available: Tell user — Zotero’s magic wand (DOI lookup) fastest path - ResearchGate link: Flag user manual check — programmatic fetch blocked (403), full text often - BC gov report: Search ACAT, .gov.bc.ca library, EIRS viewer - Paywalled: Note , move . Don’t waste time trying bypass.

title: mc
text: Preferred order: 1. DOI magic wand Zotero UI (fastest, complete metadata) 2. Web API POST collections array (grey literature, local PDFs — targets collection directly, UI interaction needed) 3. saveItems via /zotero-api (batch creation structured data — requires UI collection selection) 4. JS console script group library (connector can’t target right collection) Collection targeting: saveItems drops items whatever collection selected Zotero’s UI. Always confirm user calling . Web API bypasses — include "collections": ["KEY"] POST body. Find collection keys ?q=name search collections endpoint.

title: mc
text: saveItems attachments silently fail. Don’t use . Instead: Web API S3 upload (preferred): Create attachment item → get upload auth → build S3 body (Python: prefix + file bytes + suffix) → POST S3 → register uploadKey. Works without Zotero running. See /zotero-api skill section 4. JS console fallback: Download curl, attach via item_attach_pdf.js Zotero JS console. Verify attachment exists via MCP: zotero_get_item_children

title: mc
text: manual adds, confirm via MCP: - zotero_search_items — find title - zotero_get_item_metadata — check fields complete - zotero_get_item_children — confirm PDF attached

title: mc
text: duplicates created (common saveItems retries): - Run collection_dedup.js via Zotero JS console - keeps copy attachments, trashes rest

title: mc
text: rbbt pulls BBT, syncs Zotero. Edit references Zotero → rebuild report → bibliography updates. Library targeting: rbbt must know Zotero library search. set globally ~/.Rprofile: Without option, rbbt searches personal library (libraryID 1) won’t find group library references. library IDs map Zotero’s internal numbering — use /zotero-lookup SELECT DISTINCT libraryID citationkey BBT database discover available libraries.
code: # index.Rmd — dynamic bib from Zotero via Better BibTeX bibliography: "`r rbbt::bbt_write_bib('references.bib', overwrite = TRUE)`" # default library — NewGraphEnvironment group (libraryID 9, group 4733734) options(rbbt.default.library_id = 9)

title: mc
text: [@key2020] — parenthetical: (Author 2020) @key2020 — narrative: Author (2020) [@key1; @key2] — multiple nocite: YAML — include uncited references

title: mc
text: review paper references older study, trace back original cite . Don’t attribute findings review original exists. (See LLM Agent Conventions newgraph.md.) original unavailable (paywalled, print, can’t locate): use secondary citation format prose include bib entries sources: Smith et al. (2003; cited Doctor 2022) found … @smith2003 @doctor2022 go .bib file. reader can track original . Flag incomplete metadata primary entry — ’s better partial reference none .

title: mc
text: need PDF obvious URL doesn’t work: DOI resolver → publisher site (often OA link) Europe PMC (europepmc.org/backend/ptpmcrender.fcgi?accid=PMC{ID}&blobtype=pdf) — ncbi blocks curl SciELO — needs User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 header ResearchGate — flag user manual download Semantic Scholar — sometimes OA links Ask user institutional access Always verify downloads: file paper.pdf say “PDF document”, HTML.

title: mc
text: scripts/rag_build.R — maps citation keys Zotero PDF attachment keys, builds DuckDB data/rag/ gitignored — store local, committed Dependencies: ragnar, Ollama nomic-embed-text model See /lit-search skill full recipe

title: mc
text: ragnar_store_connect() ragnar_retrieve() — returns chunks source file attribution.

title: mc
text: NEVER write abstracts manually — CrossRef abstract, leave blank NEVER cite specific numbers without verifying source PDF via ragnar search NEVER paraphrase equations — copy exact notation cite page/section

title: Tables in Emails
text: mc_md_render() converts single markdown file HTML. need R-generated table middle email? can’t put R code plain .md file.

title: Tables in Emails
text: mc_compose() takes mix markdown files, HTML strings, kable objects stitches one email body. argument resolved order: .md file — header --- stripped, body converted HTML kable/kableExtra object — converted HTML via .character() HTML string — passed -<table>, <th>, <td> elements get border padding styles injected automatically Gmail compatibility.
code:
library(mc) df <- data.frame( Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena"), Plugs = c(4000, 3000, 3000) ) body <- mc_compose( "communications/intro.md", knitr::kable(df, format = "html"), "

Let me know if the numbers look right.

" ) mc_send(html = body, to = "brandon@example.com", subject = "Planting site summary")

title: Tables in Emails
text: reliable option. Clean HTML, minimal styling, works everywhere. mc_compose() adds border padding inline styles every cell.
code: df <- data.frame(   Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena"),   Plugs = c(4000, 3000, 3000),   Status = c("Confirmed", "Pending", "Pending") )  knitr::kable(df, format = "html")

title: Tables in Emails
text: colors, bold headers, highlighted rows, use kableExtra::row_spec() kableExtra::column_spec(). add inline styles survive Gmail’s CSS stripping. Avoid bootstrap_options like "striped" "hover" — rely CSS classes <style> block Gmail strips. Use row_spec() explicit background colors instead:
code: library(kableExtra)  kbl(df, format = "html") |>   kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) |>   row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#f5f5f5") |>   column_spec(1, bold = TRUE) |>   column_spec(2, color = "#2c7bb6") kbl(df, format = "html") |>   kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) |>   row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#f5f5f5") |>   row_spec(seq(2, nrow(df), 2), background = "#f9f9f9")

title: Tables in Emails
text: Large tables can wrapped scrollable <div> using mc_scroll(). Gmail preserves overflow CSS scroll email client.

title: Tables in Emails
code: wide_df <- data.frame(   Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena"),   Plugs = c(4000, 3000, 3000),   Season = c("Fall 2026", "Fall 2026", "Fall 2026"),   Latitude = c(53.92, 55.34, 54.38),   Longitude = c(-124.02, -122.97, -127.17),   BEC_Zone = c("SBSdw3", "SBSmk1", "SBSdk"),   Elevation_m = c(680, 700, 520),   Stock = c("Local", "Transfer", "Transfer"),   Nursery = c("PRT Vernon", "PRT Vernon", "PRT Vernon"),   Crew = c("Alpine Reforestation", "TBD", "TBD"),   Access = c("FSR 200", "FSR 440", "Morice West"),   Prep = c("Complete", "Scheduled", "Not started"),   Soil = c("Silty clay", "Sandy loam", "Clay loam"),   Moisture = c("Subhygric", "Mesic", "Hygric"),   Permit = c("Approved", "In review", "Not submitted") )  cat(mc_scroll(knitr::kable(wide_df, format = "html"), direction = "wide"))

title: Tables in Emails
code: set.seed(42) sites <- c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena", "Fraser", "Bulkley",            "Stellako", "Endako", "Stuart", "Salmon", "Bowron") long_df <- data.frame(   Row = 1:50,   Site = rep(sites, 5),   Plugs = sample(100:500, 50, replace = TRUE),   Survival_Pct = round(runif(50, 60, 98), 1),   Height_cm = round(runif(50, 15, 85), 0) )  cat(mc_scroll(knitr::kable(long_df, format = "html"), direction = "long"))

title: Tables in Emails
text: default max_height vertical scrolling "400px". Adjust mc_scroll(..., max_height = "600px").
code: set.seed(42) both_df <- data.frame(   Row = 1:30,   Site = rep(sites, 3),   Plugs = sample(100:500, 30, replace = TRUE),   Survival = round(runif(30, 60, 98), 1),   Height_cm = round(runif(30, 15, 85), 0),   BEC_Zone = rep(c("SBSdw3", "SBSmk1", "SBSdk", "SBSwk1", "SBSmc2",                    "SBSdw1", "SBSwk3", "SBSdk", "SBSmw", "SBSdw2"), 3),   Elevation = sample(400:900, 30, replace = TRUE),   Lat = round(runif(30, 53, 56), 4),   Lon = round(runif(30, -128, -122), 4),   Source = rep(c("Local", "Transfer"), 15),   Soil = rep(c("Silty clay", "Sandy loam", "Clay loam", "Silt loam", "Gravel"), 6),   Moisture = rep(c("Subhygric", "Mesic", "Hygric", "Subxeric", "Mesic"), 6),   Fish = rep(c("Yes", "Yes", "No", "Yes", "No"), 6),   Permit = rep(c("Approved", "In review", "Not submitted", "Approved", "Pending"), 6) )  cat(mc_scroll(knitr::kable(both_df, format = "html"), direction = "both"))

title: Tables in Emails
code:
library(mc) sites <- data.frame( Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena"), Plugs = c(4000, 3000, 3000), Season = c("Fall 2026", "Fall 2026", "Fall 2026") ) body <- mc_compose( "

Hi Brandon,

Here's the current planting plan:

", kableExtra::kbl(sites, format = "html") |> kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) |> kableExtra::row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#f5f5f5"), "

Total: 10,000 plugs across three sites.

Does this match what the nursery has available?

" ) mc_send(html = body, to = "brandon@example.com", subject = "2026 planting plan — cottonwood plugs")

title: Tables in Emails
text: Use send_at send email later. works mc_compose() mc_scroll() — build body now, send want. macOS, caffeinate prevents machine sleeping email sends. laptop lid can closed long power connected. machine sleep send window, 5-minute grace period applies — past send skipped prevent stale emails firing. scheduled send outcomes (sent, skipped, failed) logged ~/.mc/send_log.txt trigger macOS desktop notification.
code:
library(mc) sites <- data.frame( Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie", "Skeena"), Plugs = c(4000, 3000, 3000), Season = c("Fall 2026", "Fall 2026", "Fall 2026") ) body <- mc_compose( "

Hi Brandon,

Here's the planting plan with a scrollable table:

", mc_scroll( kableExtra::kbl(sites, format = "html") |> kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) |> kableExtra::row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#f5f5f5"), direction = "both" ), "

Let me know if the numbers look right.

" ) # Send in 30 minutes proc <- mc_send(html = body, to = "brandon@example.com", subject = "2026 planting plan", send_at = 30) # Or at a specific time mc_send(html = body, to = "brandon@example.com", subject = "2026 planting plan", send_at = as.POSIXct("2026-02-24 09:00:00")) # Check or cancel proc$is_alive() proc$kill()

title: Authors and Citation
text: Allan Irvine. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Irvine (2026). mc: Mail Composer - Email Composition Delivery. R package version 0.2.9, https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/mc.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mc: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery},   author = {Allan Irvine},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.9},   url = {https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/mc}, }

what: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Mail Composer. Three lines draft email. Compose, draft, send emails markdown files via Gmail API. Wraps gmailr eliminate 80 lines boilerplate every email script repeats.

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
code: pak::pak("NewGraphEnvironment/mc")

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Set default sender address ~/.Rprofile: via environment variable ~/.Renviron: authenticate per machine:
code: options(mc.from = "you@example.com") MC_FROM=you@example.com mc_auth()

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Write email body markdown. Everything --- separator human-readable envelope (notes author) stripped mc_md_render() conversion. Recipients, subject, envelope fields set R parameters mc_send(). Send three lines: creates Gmail draft HTML formatting standard signature appended. Authentication happens automatically via cached OAuth tokens (run mc_auth() per machine set ). ’re ready send real:
code: # Email to Brandon - Cottonwood  **Subject:** Cottonwood plugs - 2026 planting **To:** brandon@example.com  ---  Hi Brandon,  Quick question about the cottonwood plugs.  Thanks, Al library(mc) mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs - 2026 planting") mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         draft = FALSE)

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Need R-generated table email? Use mc_compose() mix markdown files, HTML, kable/kableExtra objects: Large tables? Wrap mc_scroll() horizontal/vertical scrolling: See vignette("tables--emails") kableExtra styling scrolling examples.
code:
df <- data.frame(Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie"), Plugs = c(4000, 3000)) body <- mc_compose( "communications/intro.md", knitr::kable(df, format = "html"), "

Let me know if this looks right.

" ) mc_send(html = body, to = "brandon@example.com", subject = "Planting plan") mc_compose( "

Full dataset:

", mc_scroll(knitr::kable(big_df, format = "html"), direction = "both") )

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Full function documentation examples: newgraphenvironment.github.io/mc

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Reply existing conversation: Note: gm_create_draft() support thread_id. Drafts always standalone. Use draft = FALSE send directly thread, send draft manually Gmail UI (Gmail match subject line starts “Re:”).
code: # Find the thread mc_thread_find("from:brandon subject:cottonwood")  # Read the conversation to review context mc_thread_read("19c05f0a98188c91")  # Include drafts in the thread (adds a status column: "sent" / "draft") mc_thread_read("19c05f0a98188c91", drafts = TRUE)  # Send into it mc_send("draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Re: Cottonwood plugs",         thread_id = "19c05f0a98188c91",         draft = FALSE)

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Send email later send_at — either minutes now specific time: macOS, caffeinate keeps machine awake email sends. laptop lid can closed long power connected. machine sleeps send window (e.g., power loss), 5-minute grace period applies — past , send skipped prevent stale emails. Outcomes logged ~/.mc/send_log.txt trigger macOS desktop notification success, skip, failure.
code: # Send in 10 minutes proc <- mc_send("draft.md",                 to = "brandon@example.com",                 subject = "Cottonwood plugs",                 send_at = 10)  # Send at a specific time mc_send("draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         send_at = as.POSIXct("2026-02-24 09:11:00"))  # Check status or cancel proc$is_alive() proc$kill()

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Send preview: Test mode redirects address, strips CC/BCC, ignores thread_id.
code: mc_send("draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         test = TRUE)

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: MIT

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
text: Wrapper around gmailr::gm_auth() default New Graph email address. Call per session mc_send().

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
code: mc_auth(email = default_from())

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
text: email Email address authenticate . Default uses getOption("mc."), MC_FROM environment variable. Errors neither set.

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
text: Invisible NULL. Called side effect authenticating.

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mc_auth()  # Set globally in .Rprofile to avoid passing email every time: options(mc.from = "you@example.com") } # }

what: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
text: Combines markdown files, HTML strings, kable/kableExtra table objects single HTML email body. Use need R-generated tables dynamic content mixed prose.

title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
code: mc_compose(..., sig = TRUE, sig_path = NULL)

title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
text: ... Parts compose, order. can : Markdown file path .md file path. header --- stripped (present) body converted HTML. HTML string character string raw HTML, passed -. kable/kableExtra object Output knitr::kable() kableExtra functions. Converted character automatically. sig Logical. Append signature? Default TRUE. sig_path Path custom signature HTML file. Default NULL.

title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
text: character string HTML ready html argument mc_send().

title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
text: tables look good email: Use knitr::kable() clean, simple tables Use kableExtra::row_spec() kableExtra::column_spec() inline styling (colors, bold, backgrounds) Avoid bootstrap_options like "striped" — CSS classes stripped Gmail Wrap large tables mc_scroll() horizontal/vertical scrolling mc_compose() automatically adds border padding inline styles <table>, <th>, <td> elements Gmail compatibility.

title: Compose an HTML email from mixed content — mc_compose
code:
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Prose + table + more prose df <- data.frame(Site = c("Nechako", "Mackenzie"), Plugs = c(4000, 3000)) body <- mc_compose( "communications/intro.md", knitr::kable(df, format = "html"), "communications/closing.md" ) mc_send(html = body, to = "someone@example.com", subject = "Update") # Inline markdown (no file needed) body <- mc_compose( "

Hi Brandon,

", knitr::kable(df, format = "html"), "

Let me know if this looks right.

" ) } # }

what: Ensure Gmail user labels exist (create any missing) — mc_label_ensure
title: Ensure Gmail user labels exist (create any missing) — mc_label_ensure
text: Looks existing user labels via gmailr::gm_labels() creates name label_names yet exist. -op labels already present. System label names (INBOX, STARRED, etc.) skipped — built Gmail created. System-label match case-insensitive "Inbox", "inbox", "INBOX" skipped.

title: Ensure Gmail user labels exist (create any missing) — mc_label_ensure
code: mc_label_ensure(label_names)

title: Ensure Gmail user labels exist (create any missing) — mc_label_ensure
text: label_names Character vector Gmail label names ensure exist.

title: Ensure Gmail user labels exist (create any missing) — mc_label_ensure
text: Invisibly returns label_names.

title: Ensure Gmail user labels exist (create any missing) — mc_label_ensure
text: Useful YAML-driven label workflow mc_send() / mc_md_send(): new project tags can land frontmatter without needing created Gmail UI first. Also handy seeding project's full label set front.

title: Ensure Gmail user labels exist (create any missing) — mc_label_ensure
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Tag-as-you-go: works even if "project-foo" doesn't exist yet mc_label_ensure(c("project-foo", "urgent")) mc_thread_modify("18171fb2cec08e9d", add = c("project-foo", "urgent"))  # Seed a project's labels in one shot mc_label_ensure(c("client/Acme", "client/Acme/in-flight",                   "client/Acme/done")) } # }

what: Index markdown email drafts under a directory — mc_md_index
title: Index markdown email drafts under a directory — mc_md_index
text: Scans directory tree markdown drafts matching pattern returns data frame summarising frontmatter. Turns compost-style communications/ folder searchable archive without querying Gmail.

title: Index markdown email drafts under a directory — mc_md_index
code: mc_md_index(   dir = "communications",   pattern = "_draft\\.md$",   recursive = TRUE )

title: Index markdown email drafts under a directory — mc_md_index
text: dir Directory scan. Defaults "communications". pattern Regex matched filename (path). Default "_draft\\.md$" matches compost draft naming. recursive Logical. Recurse subdirectories? Default TRUE.

title: Index markdown email drafts under a directory — mc_md_index
text: data frame columns path, date, , cc, subject, thread_id, has_attachments. date parsed 8-digit YYYYMMDD_ prefix basename present, else NA. Multi-value fields (, cc) collapsed ", ".

title: Index markdown email drafts under a directory — mc_md_index
text: Files without YAML frontmatter included NA metadata columns missing drafts visible index rather hidden.

title: Index markdown email drafts under a directory — mc_md_index
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mc_md_index("communications/") mc_md_index() |> dplyr::filter(grepl("cindy", to)) } # }

what: Read YAML frontmatter from a markdown email draft — mc_md_meta
title: Read YAML frontmatter from a markdown email draft — mc_md_meta
text: Parses YAML frontmatter block top markdown file returns named list. Returns empty list file frontmatter.

title: Read YAML frontmatter from a markdown email draft — mc_md_meta
code: mc_md_meta(path)

title: Read YAML frontmatter from a markdown email draft — mc_md_meta
text: path Path markdown file.

title: Read YAML frontmatter from a markdown email draft — mc_md_meta
text: named list frontmatter fields.

title: Read YAML frontmatter from a markdown email draft — mc_md_meta
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mc_md_meta("communications/20260413_cindy_newsletter_draft.md") } # }

what: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: Reads markdown file, strips everything first --- separator (header metadata), converts remaining markdown HTML, inlines table styles Gmail compatibility, appends New Graph signature.

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
code: mc_md_render(path, sig = TRUE, sig_path = NULL)

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: path Path markdown draft file. sig Logical. Append signature? Default TRUE. sig_path Path custom signature HTML file. Default NULL uses bundled New Graph signature. Ignored sig = FALSE.

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: character string HTML ready mc_send().

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: Gmail strips <style> blocks HTML emails. function adds inline styles <table>, <th>, <td> elements tables render correctly. markdown file follow compost template format:   Everything including --- line stripped. Everything converted HTML.
code: # Email to Recipient - Topic  **Subject:** ...  **To:** ...  ---  Hi Name,  Body text here.

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ html <- mc_md_render("communications/20260222_brandon_cottonwood_draft.md") cat(html) } # }

what: Send or draft an email from a markdown file with YAML frontmatter — mc_md_send
title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file with YAML frontmatter — mc_md_send
text: One-file workflow: reads metadata (, subject, optional cc, bcc, thread_id, attachments, labels, ) YAML frontmatter top markdown draft dispatches mc_send(). Lets callers keep logical email single .md file instead splitting subject, body, recipients across paired .R script.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file with YAML frontmatter — mc_md_send
code: mc_md_send(path, draft = TRUE, test = FALSE, override = list())

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file with YAML frontmatter — mc_md_send
text: path Path markdown draft (YAML frontmatter). draft Logical. TRUE (default), create Gmail draft. test Logical. Test mode — sends self, strips cc/thread_id. override Named list arguments override frontmatter values call time (e.g. list(draft = FALSE) send). Overrides merge frontmatter, override wins.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file with YAML frontmatter — mc_md_send
text: Invisibly returns whatever mc_send() returns.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file with YAML frontmatter — mc_md_send
text: Required frontmatter fields: , subject. Missing either triggers error names file.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file with YAML frontmatter — mc_md_send
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Draft from a frontmattered .md mc_md_send("communications/20260413_cindy_newsletter_draft.md")  # Send for real, overriding the default draft = TRUE mc_md_send(   "communications/20260413_cindy_newsletter_draft.md",   override = list(draft = FALSE) ) } # }

what: Search Gmail at the message level — mc_message_find
title: Search Gmail at the message level — mc_message_find
text: Like mc_thread_find() returns one row per message rather per thread. Useful timed sends, re-threads, manually moved drafts scatter logical conversation across multiple thread IDs want locate specific message directly.

title: Search Gmail at the message level — mc_message_find
code: mc_message_find(   query,   n = 10,   after = NULL,   before = NULL,   status = c("any", "sent", "draft") )

title: Search Gmail at the message level — mc_message_find
text: query Gmail search query. n Maximum number results. Default 10. , Optional date filters. Date "YYYY-MM-DD" string. See mc_thread_find(). status One "" (default), "sent", "draft". "sent" restricts non-draft messages; "draft" searches drafts instead messages.

title: Search Gmail at the message level — mc_message_find
text: data frame columns message_id, thread_id, , , subject, date, status, recent first. Returns empty data frame (columns) messages match.

title: Search Gmail at the message level — mc_message_find
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mc_message_find("to:cindy newsletter", after = Sys.Date() - 1) mc_message_find("subject:invoice", status = "sent") mc_message_find("subject:draft-only", status = "draft") } # }

what: Preview composed HTML in a browser before sending — mc_preview
title: Preview composed HTML in a browser before sending — mc_preview
text: Writes composed HTML local file opens default browser markdown rendering issues (mis-nested links, stray backticks, unintended list formatting, signature layout) can caught creating Gmail draft.

title: Preview composed HTML in a browser before sending — mc_preview
code: mc_preview(   html,   path = file.path(tools::R_user_dir("mc", "cache"), "preview.html"),   open = TRUE )

title: Preview composed HTML in a browser before sending — mc_preview
text: html Either character string HTML (e.g. mc_compose()) path .md draft file. .md path detected combination endsWith(x, ".md") file.exists(x). path File path write preview . Defaults stable location tools::R_user_dir() (mc/cache/preview.html) file persists R session exits can opened manually. open Logical. TRUE (default), open file utils::browseURL(). FALSE, write file.

title: Preview composed HTML in a browser before sending — mc_preview
text: file path, invisibly.

title: Preview composed HTML in a browser before sending — mc_preview
text: Accepts either raw HTML string path .md draft. .md path passed, frontmatter envelope (/ Cc / Subject / Thread / Attachments) rendered body recipient subject mistakes visible .

title: Preview composed HTML in a browser before sending — mc_preview
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # From raw HTML html <- mc_compose("communications/project/draft.md") mc_preview(html)  # Directly from a frontmattered .md — shows envelope above body mc_preview("communications/20260413_cindy_newsletter_draft.md") } # }

what: Wrap a table in a scrollable container for email — mc_scroll
title: Wrap a table in a scrollable container for email — mc_scroll
text: Wraps HTML table output <div> overflow CSS large tables scroll horizontally, vertically, Gmail.

title: Wrap a table in a scrollable container for email — mc_scroll
code: mc_scroll(table, direction = "both", max_height = "400px")

title: Wrap a table in a scrollable container for email — mc_scroll
text: table kable/kableExtra object character string HTML. direction Scroll direction: "wide" (horizontal), "long" (vertical), "". Default "". max_height Maximum height vertical scrolling kicks . Default "400px". Ignored direction = "wide".

title: Wrap a table in a scrollable container for email — mc_scroll
text: character string HTML table inside scrollable div.

title: Wrap a table in a scrollable container for email — mc_scroll
code:
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- data.frame(x = 1:50, y = rnorm(50)) # Vertical scroll mc_scroll(knitr::kable(df, format = "html"), direction = "long") # Horizontal scroll mc_scroll(knitr::kable(wide_df, format = "html"), direction = "wide") # Use with mc_compose body <- mc_compose( "

Here's the data:

", mc_scroll(knitr::kable(df, format = "html")) ) } # }

what: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: main function. Reads markdown draft, renders HTML, builds MIME message, either creates Gmail draft sends immediately.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
code: mc_send(   path = NULL,   to,   subject,   cc = NULL,   bcc = NULL,   from = default_from(),   thread_id = NULL,   draft = TRUE,   test = FALSE,   sig = TRUE,   sig_path = NULL,   attachments = NULL,   labels = NULL,   labels_create = TRUE,   html = NULL,   send_at = NULL )

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: path Path markdown draft file. Passed mc_md_render(). Recipient email address (character string vector). subject Email subject line. cc Optional CC recipients (character vector). Default NULL. bcc Optional BCC recipients (character vector). Default NULL. Sender address. Default uses getOption("mc."), MC_FROM environment variable. Errors neither set. thread_id Gmail thread ID reply . Default NULL (new thread). Use mc_thread_find() look thread IDs. draft Logical. TRUE (default), create Gmail draft. FALSE, send immediately. test Logical. TRUE, override (send self) ignore cc thread_id. Default FALSE. sig Logical. Append signature? Passed mc_md_render(). Default TRUE. sig_path Path custom signature HTML file. Default NULL uses bundled New Graph signature. Passed mc_md_render(). Ignored sig = FALSE html provided. attachments Optional character vector file paths attach. file attached via gmailr::gm_attach_file(). Default NULL. labels Optional character vector Gmail label names apply resulting thread. Applied via mc_thread_modify() successful draft creation send. Labels applied draft's thread draft path; cases Gmail keeps thread draft sent UI, labels carry . labels_create Logical. TRUE (default), missing user labels labels auto-created via mc_label_ensure() applied — supports tag---go YAML-driven drafts. Set FALSE keep typo-guard behavior (errors unknown labels, downgraded warning per existing label tryCatch). html Optional pre-rendered HTML body. provided, path ignored HTML used directly. send_at Schedule email later. Either POSIXct datetime numeric number minutes now. Default NULL (send/draft immediately). set, draft forced FALSE email sent background R process via callr::r_bg(). Requires callr package.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: send_at NULL, Gmail thread ID resulting draft sent message, returned invisibly. May NULL gmailr response include one (e.g. mocked tests). send_at set, returns callr::r_bg() process handle invisibly. Use $is_alive() check status $kill() cancel.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: Authenticates automatically active Gmail session detected.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: Gmail's gm_create_draft() support thread_id. draft = TRUE thread_id set, mc_send() issues warning draft appear thread manually sent Gmail UI. Set subject "Re: Original Subject" Gmail's thread-matching heuristic can place correctly. draft = FALSE thread_id set, message sent directly thread via gm_send_message(thread_id = ...).

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: test = TRUE sends , strips CC, ignores thread_id prevent accidental sends real threads development.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: send_at runs background R process machine. macOS, caffeinate used prevent idle sleep machine stays awake email sends. laptop lid can closed long power connected. Laptop powered — sends time (caffeinate prevents sleep) Laptop powered — process dies, email never sends caffeinate bypassed machine sleeps send window, 5-minute grace period applies. Past , send skipped prevent stale emails firing unexpectedly.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a draft (safe default) mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs")  # Send into an existing thread mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Re: Cottonwood plugs",         thread_id = "19c05f0a98188c91",         draft = FALSE)  # Test mode — sends to self mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         test = TRUE)  # Send in 10 minutes proc <- mc_send("communications/draft.md",                 to = "brandon@example.com",                 subject = "Cottonwood plugs",                 send_at = 10) proc$is_alive()  # check if still waiting proc$kill()      # cancel  # Send at a specific time mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         send_at = as.POSIXct("2026-02-24 09:11:00"))  # Attach files mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Planting plan",         attachments = c("data/plan.xlsx", "fig/map.pdf")) } # }

what: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
title: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
text: Reads signature HTML file. Defaults standard New Graph signature bundled inst/sig/signature.html. Pass custom path use different signature.

title: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
code: mc_sig(path = NULL)

title: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
text: path Path signature HTML file. Default NULL uses bundled New Graph signature.

title: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
text: character string HTML.

title: Return an email signature as HTML — mc_sig
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cat(mc_sig()) cat(mc_sig("path/to/custom_sig.html")) } # }

what: Return the latest top-level message body in a thread — mc_thread_body_latest
title: Return the latest top-level message body in a thread — mc_thread_body_latest
text: Convenience wrapper mc_thread_read() pulls recent message thread , default, strips quoted reply history get just actually written top. Useful recording sent, comparing draft vs sent, scanning thread activity.

title: Return the latest top-level message body in a thread — mc_thread_body_latest
code: mc_thread_body_latest(   thread_id,   strip_quotes = TRUE,   status = c("any", "sent", "draft") )

title: Return the latest top-level message body in a thread — mc_thread_body_latest
text: thread_id Gmail thread ID. strip_quotes Logical. TRUE (default), remove lines starting > plus "... wrote:" attribution line Gmail inserts quoted history. status One "" (default), "sent", "draft". Restricts pool messages considered selecting latest.

title: Return the latest top-level message body in a thread — mc_thread_body_latest
text: single character string latest body ("" none).

title: Return the latest top-level message body in a thread — mc_thread_body_latest
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mc_thread_body_latest("19cd3565c3161b4b") mc_thread_body_latest("19cd3565c3161b4b", strip_quotes = FALSE) } # }

what: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
text: Searches Gmail messages returns matching thread IDs. Useful finding thread_id pass mc_send() replying existing conversation.

title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
code: mc_thread_find(query, n = 5, after = NULL, before = NULL)

title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
text: query Gmail search query. Supports syntax Gmail search box (e.g., ":brandon subject:cottonwood"). n Maximum number results. Default 5. , Optional date filters. Date object character string "YYYY-MM-DD" form. Translated Gmail's : / : operators (inclusive/exclusive semantics follow Gmail's behaviour: : inclusive, : exclusive).

title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
text: data frame columns thread_id, , subject, date, sorted recent first.

title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mc_thread_find("from:brandon.geldart subject:cottonwood") mc_thread_find("from:brandon newer_than:7d") mc_thread_find("newsletter", after = Sys.Date() - 7) } # }

what: Modify a Gmail thread's label state (archive, trash, star, label, etc.) — mc_thread_modify
title: Modify a Gmail thread's label state (archive, trash, star, label, etc.) — mc_thread_modify
text: Adds /removes labels thread one call. Accepts user-defined label names Gmail system labels (INBOX, STARRED, UNREAD, IMPORTANT, TRASH, SPAM, SENT, DRAFT). Covers common operations beyond "labeling": archive (remove = "INBOX"), star (add = "STARRED"), trash (add = "TRASH"), mark-read (remove = "UNREAD").

title: Modify a Gmail thread's label state (archive, trash, star, label, etc.) — mc_thread_modify
code: mc_thread_modify(thread_id, add = NULL, remove = NULL, create_missing = FALSE)

title: Modify a Gmail thread's label state (archive, trash, star, label, etc.) — mc_thread_modify
text: thread_id Gmail thread ID (e.g. mc_thread_find()). add Character vector label names add. NULL none. remove Character vector label names remove. NULL none. create_missing Logical. TRUE, calls mc_label_ensure() add resolving names — user label add yet exist created. Defaults FALSE (strict: errors unknown labels) existing callers keep typo guard.

title: Modify a Gmail thread's label state (archive, trash, star, label, etc.) — mc_thread_modify
text: Invisibly returns gmailr response.

title: Modify a Gmail thread's label state (archive, trash, star, label, etc.) — mc_thread_modify
text: Pass names, IDs. User-label names resolve opaque IDs via gmailr::gm_labels(). System labels pass unchanged. user label's name collides system label ID (e.g. user label named "STARRED"), system interpretation wins — almost always intended. least one add remove must non-NULL. gm_labels() call skipped entirely every input system label. Set create_missing = TRUE applying labels YAML-driven workflow (e.g. via mc_md_send()) new project tags error first use. See mc_label_ensure() underlying primitive.

title: Modify a Gmail thread's label state (archive, trash, star, label, etc.) — mc_thread_modify
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Apply a user label mc_thread_modify("18171fb2cec08e9d", add = "Clients/Acme")  # Archive and mark read in one call mc_thread_modify("18171fb2cec08e9d", remove = c("INBOX", "UNREAD"))  # Star a thread mc_thread_modify("18171fb2cec08e9d", add = "STARRED")  # Status transition mc_thread_modify("18171fb2cec08e9d", add = "Done", remove = "Pending") } # }

what: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
title: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
text: Fetches thread ID returns message's sender, date, subject, plain-text body. Useful reviewing conversation composing follow-mc_send().

title: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
code: mc_thread_read(thread_id, drafts = FALSE)

title: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
text: thread_id Gmail thread ID (mc_thread_find()). drafts Logical. TRUE, also include draft messages output add status column ("sent" "draft"). Default FALSE backwards compatibility.

title: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
text: data frame columns , date, subject, body, ordered oldest newest. drafts = TRUE, additional status column included.

title: Read all messages in a Gmail thread — mc_thread_read
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mc_thread_find("from:brandon subject:cottonwood") mc_thread_read("19adb18351867c34") mc_thread_read("19adb18351867c34", drafts = TRUE) } # }

title: mc 0.2.9
text: Add mc_label_ensure(label_names) — primitive creates missing Gmail user labels -ops existing ones. System labels (INBOX, STARRED, etc.) skipped via case-insensitive match (#33). mc_thread_modify() gains create_missing = FALSE (opt-). TRUE, calls mc_label_ensure(add) resolving names new labels add auto-created. Default preserves existing typo-guard behavior (#33). mc_send() gains labels_create = TRUE (default ). YAML- driven workflow (mc_md_send()) now creates new project tags first use — need pre-create labels via gmailr::gm_create_label(). Set FALSE strict typo-guard. Threaded scheduled-send recursion (#33). Internal: resolve_label_names() now matches system labels case- insensitively normalizes returned IDs uppercase, fixing cross-function inconsistency mc_label_ensure skipped "inbox" system mc_thread_modify erred trying resolve . Side benefit: callers can pass mixed-case system labels (“Inbox”, “Starred”) get correct resolution.

title: mc 0.2.8
text: mc_send() accepts labels argument (character vector Gmail label names) applies resulting thread via mc_thread_modify() draft sent paths. Drafts get tagged ’re findable Drafts project label label rides user sends Gmail UI (Gmail typically keeps thread). Unknown label names raise existing mc_thread_modify() error listing available user labels (#31). mc_md_send() reads labels: YAML frontmatter (character vector) passes mc_send(). Project tags can live .md draft alongside , subject, cc, thread_id, etc. (#31). mc_send() now returns Gmail thread ID resulting draft sent message invisibly (invisible(NULL)). Lets callers chain follow-operations like mc_thread_modify() cleanly (#31).

title: mc 0.2.7
text: Add mc_thread_modify() — adds /removes labels Gmail thread one call. Accepts user label names Gmail system labels (INBOX, STARRED, UNREAD, IMPORTANT, TRASH, SPAM, SENT, DRAFT), verb covers archive (remove = "INBOX"), star (add = "STARRED"), trash, mark-read, project-label workflows (#28). Bypasses body-encoding bug gmailr::gm_modify_thread() (3.0.0) POSTing directly users.threads.modify. httr added Imports.

title: mc 0.2.6
text: Startup quote ritual: library(mc) prints random fact-checked quote 25 voices attach. Italic quote, grey attribution, clickable blue source hyperlink. Suppress via options(mc.quote_show_source = FALSE). 99 shipped entries MLK, JFK, Bob Marley, Kurt Cobain, Chris Cornell, Robert Plant, James Hetfield, Tupac, Eminem, Ice-T, Ice Cube, RZA, ODB, Method Man, DJ Premier, Vince Staples, Logic, Mike Tyson, Jim Carrey, John Candy, Ronny Chieng, Jon Stewart, Stephen King, Quentin Tarantino, Paul Thomas Anderson. Curated via soul /quotes-enable skill: parallel research agents, independent fact-check pass, user review. cli added Imports OSC 8 hyperlinks styling R/zzz.R.

title: mc 0.2.5
text: mc_preview() now accepts .md path addition HTML string. given path, renders frontmatter envelope (/ Cc / Subject / Thread / Attach) header body recipient subject mistakes visible locally (#25).

title: mc 0.2.4
text: Add YAML frontmatter support one-file email drafts (#23). Add mc_md_meta() read frontmatter named list. Add mc_md_send() dispatch mc_send() frontmatter, override arg call-time tweaks. Add mc_md_index() scan directory tree drafts return searchable dataframe (path, date, , cc, subject, thread_id, has_attachments).

title: mc 0.2.3
text: mc_preview() now writes stable path tools::R_user_dir("mc","cache") instead tempfile() preview survives non-interactive Rscript sessions. open now defaults TRUE path configurable (#22).

title: mc 0.2.2
text: Add mc_message_find() message-level Gmail search. Add mc_thread_body_latest() — latest thread reply quoted history stripped. Add mc_preview() — preview composed HTML browser sending. Add / date filters mc_thread_find().

title: mc 0.2.1
text: Add drafts parameter mc_thread_read() — includes draft messages status column ("sent" / "draft").

title: mc 0.2.0
text: Add attachments parameter mc_send() file attachments. Remove hardcoded fallback email default_from() — now errors options(mc.) MC_FROM env var unset. Update CLAUDE.md latest soul conventions.

title: mc 0.1.0
text: First stable release: compose, draft, send emails markdown via Gmail API. Core functions: mc_send(), mc_compose(), mc_md_render(), mc_scroll(), mc_sig(). Thread support: mc_thread_find(), mc_thread_read(). Scheduled send send_at macOS caffeinate integration. Test mode redirects sender safe previewing.
